steps.reveal: Me = me (acc., subject of future infinitive in indirect discourse)

hints.nominative: 'Ego' is nominative — 'Me' is accusative, subject of the infinitive 'satisfacturum [esse]'
hints.ablative: 'Me' can be ablative, but here it is accusative subject of indirect discourse

hints.one with: 'Una' here is an adverb meaning 'together,' not the number 'one' as an adjective

steps.reveal: meis liberis = with my children (abl., governed by 'cum')

hints.nominative: Nominative plural would be 'mei liberi' — 'meis liberis' is ablative with 'cum'
hints.accusative: Accusative plural would be 'meos liberos' — 'meis liberis' is ablative
hints.dative: 'Liberis' could be dative plural, but 'cum' requires the ablative

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive plural in -arum — 'liberorum' ends in -orum
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive plural in -um/-ium — 'liberorum' ends in -orum
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive plural in -uum — 'liberorum' ends in -orum

steps.reveal: rei publicae = for the republic (dat., with 'satisfacere')

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'res publica' — 'rei publicae' is genitive or dative
hints.genitive: 'Rei publicae' could be genitive, but 'satisfacere' takes a dative object
hints.ablative: Ablative would be 're publica' — 'rei publicae' is dative with 'satisfacere'

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'rei' ends in -ei
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'rei' ends in -ei
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'rei' ends in -ei

hints.to do enough: 'To do enough' is a literal rendering — 'satisfacere' idiomatically means to satisfy or make amends
hints.to sacrifice: 'To sacrifice' is 'sacrificare' — 'satisfacere' means to give satisfaction
hints.to make: 'To make' is 'facere' — 'satisfacere' is a compound meaning to satisfy

steps.reveal: satisfacturum [esse] = will satisfy (fut. act. inf. in indirect discourse)

hints.perfect passive participle: Perfect passive participle would be 'satisfactum' — '-urum' marks the future active participle
hints.gerundive: Gerundive would be 'satisfaciendum' — '-urum' is future active participle
hints.present active participle: Present active participle would be 'satisfacientem' — '-urum' is future

hints.facio, facere, feci, factum: These are the parts of 'facere' (to make) — the base verb, but 'satisfacere' is the compound
hints.sacrifico, sacrificare, sacrificavi, sac: These are the parts of 'sacrificare' (to sacrifice) — different compound
hints.proficio, proficere, profeci, profectum: These are the parts of 'proficere' (to advance) — different compound

steps.prompt: What is the full construction of this indirect statement?
steps.target: Me...satisfacturum [esse] — future infinitive with accusative subject
choices: Me...satisfacturum [esse] — future infinitive with accusative subject

hints.purpose clause with ut: No 'ut' is present — this is acc. + future infinitive (indirect discourse)
hints.result clause with ut: No 'ut' is present — this is an indirect statement with future reference
hints.relative clause of characteristic: No relative pronoun is present — this is indirect discourse

steps.prompt: The future infinitive 'satisfacturum esse' indicates what time relation to the implied main verb?
steps.target: action subsequent to the time of the main verb
choices: action subsequent to the time of the main verb
action contemporaneous with the main verb
action prior to the main verb

hints.action contemporaneous with the main ver: Present infinitive would express contemporaneous action — future infinitive is subsequent
hints.action prior to the main verb: Perfect infinitive expresses prior action — future infinitive is subsequent
hints.no time relation: Infinitives in indirect discourse always express relative time — future = subsequent

translation: (That) I, together with my children, will make amends to the republic
